title: cysticapnos cracca
intro: Cysticapnos cracca, also known as winter's cork or voodoo poppy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Papaveraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce labor and stimulate menstruation due to its high concentration of alkaloids, particularly isopelletierine and cytisine. The health benefits of Cysticapnos cracca include its potential to treat menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and uterine prolapse. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although its use is not recommended without medical supervision due to its potential toxicity.
